commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `runReplyAgent` so source-preserving fallback runs can emit the current run's fallback notice without persisting or clearing sticky user-facing fallback state, and updates the e2e coverage for internal subagent announces.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +49. Total +55 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main gates fallback notice generation on `!preserveUserFacingSessionState`, and the current e2e test asserts no `Model Fallback:` payload for `subagent_announce`. I did not run a live Z.AI/provider failure path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the code change is narrow, but real behavior proof and fallback-notice visibility policy are maintainer decisions rather than a mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): surface preserved fallback notices
target.body:
## Summary

- Decouple model fallback notice emission from user-facing fallback-state persistence for source-preserving inter-session/subagent handoffs.
- Preserve-mode runs now emit the current run's fallback notice when the run records fallback attempts, even if the user-facing session already has the same sticky fallback pair.
- Keep sticky fallback state writes and fallback-cleared notices gated to non-preserving user-facing runs.

Refs #94919.

## Root Cause

`runReplyAgent` used `fallbackTransition.fallbackTransitioned` for both persisted fallback-state changes and visible fallback notices. That transition flag is derived from the user-facing session's stored `fallbackNotice*` fields. For source-preserving provenance such as `subagent_announce`, OpenClaw intentionally does not mutate those fields, so fallback notices could be suppressed or buried when the source run fell back.

## Verification

- `pnpm openclaw --help`
- `pnpm openclaw agent --help`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- One Codex autoreview pass found a preserve-mode sticky-state suppression gap; this patch includes the follow-up fix before opening the draft.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Source-preserving inter-session/subagent fallback runs now return the `Model Fallback` notice for the current fallback run without mutating the user-facing session's fallback/model override state.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `8a8a3a8`, with repository dependencies installed via `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, run through the actual OpenClaw CLI entrypoint after the patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw --help`; `pnpm openclaw agent --help`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the patched OpenClaw CLI:

```text
$ pnpm openclaw --help
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs --help

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw [options] [command]

Commands:
  agent                Run one agent turn via the Gateway
  status               Show Gateway, channel, model, and recent-session status
  plugins *            Install, enable, disable, and inspect plugins

$ pnpm openclaw agent --help
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --help

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw agent [options]

Run an agent turn via the Gateway (use --local for embedded)
```

The behavior-specific proof on the same PR head is the focused `runReplyAgent` regression coverage for `subagent_announce`: it drives the source-preserving fallback path with no stored fallback state and with a preexisting matching stored fallback pair, then asserts `Model Fallback` payload delivery, `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` metadata, unchanged stored fallback state, no usage line, and one lifecycle fallback event.

Observed result after fix: The patched OpenClaw CLI launched successfully from this checkout and reported version `2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8)`. The focused regression run completed with `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 61 passed (61)`. `git diff --check` produced no output.

What was not tested: Live Z.AI `ECONNRESET` behavior against a real Z.AI account/provider and the separate per-provider transport connection/serialization knob requested in #94919; that remains a product/security decision surface outside this patch.
target.author: monkeyleet
